name: Chicken Thigh

identityLock.variant: plain raw chicken thigh, meat and skin
disambiguationNotes: Canonical FoodRanked wholefood meat entries use raw values.
This baseline is currently locked to the best available plain raw chicken-thigh row in hand: meat and skin, raw.
Do not substitute cooked, roasted, fried, breaded, marinated, or heavily processed chicken-thigh values.
If skinless raw thigh is later added, keep it as an explicit separate entry rather than rewriting this baseline invisibly.

secondarySources.note: Cooked roasted thigh meat-only row exists but should no longer be used as the canonical wholefood entry because wholefoods now must use raw values. Keep only as a research note for future prepared-state comparisons.

changeNotes: Created to replace the prior cooked chicken-thigh wholefood draft after the project rule was clarified: cooked variations of wholefoods should be removed from the canonical lane.
Canonical raw baseline is now locked to USDA fdc:172385 for chicken thigh meat and skin, raw.
If the project later wants a skinless raw chicken-thigh entry, that should be added explicitly as a separate canonical food rather than replacing this baseline silently.

kcal.note: Canonical energy value for chicken thigh meat and skin, raw.

fat_g.note: Canonical fat value for chicken thigh meat and skin, raw.

protein_g.note: Canonical protein value for chicken thigh meat and skin, raw.

carb_g.note: Raw plain chicken thigh is treated as 0 g carbohydrate in the FoodRanked wholefood model and aligns with the attached raw USDA baseline.

starch_g.note: Displayed as 0 because plain raw meat does not contribute starch.

fibre_g.note: Displayed as 0 because plain raw meat does not contribute fibre.

sugar_g.note: Displayed as 0 because plain raw meat does not contribute sugar in the project display model.

omega3_mg.note: Conservative omega-3 total for the attached raw chicken-thigh baseline; keep as approximate total from the locked row family rather than a hand-wavy placeholder.

glycemic_index.note: Displayed as N/A because GI is not a meaningful metric for this raw wholefood meat entry.

collagen_g.note: Displayed as N/A until a defensible sourced method is attached for this exact raw chicken-thigh identity.

essential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A until the project adopts a defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method for this exact identity.

nonessential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A until the project adopts a defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method for this exact identity.

bioavailability_percent.note: Displayed as N/A until the project adopts a defensible sourced method for this exact raw chicken-thigh identity.

pros.title: protein quality is one of the clearest strengths
pros.explanation: Chicken thigh's main case comes from protein usefulness and remains one of the clearest reasons to score it well within the meat category.

evidence.sourceName: animal-protein quality literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Keep as a supported direction, but the file still needs the exact production citation bundle plus a locked raw nutrient row.

cons.title: fat quality is weaker than the leanest meat options
cons.explanation: Chicken thigh is still a solid protein food, but it carries more fat baggage than the leanest cuts and should not be framed like an ultra-lean meat.

evidence.sourceName: editorial judgment informed by locked raw-row profile
evidence.citationNote: Context caution informed by the now-locked raw nutrient row; keep modestly weighted.

primaryRemainingBlockers: Keep cooked chicken-thigh variants out of the canonical wholefood lane.
Add a separate skinless-raw entry later only if the project wants that as a distinct food identity.
Keep collagen_g, amino-acid score fields, and bioavailability_percent as explicit N/A until a defensible reusable method is adopted.

sourceNotes: Canonical wholefood chicken-thigh entry now uses raw values, not cooked variations.
Current primary source anchor is USDA FoodData Central fdc:172385 for chicken thigh meat and skin, raw.
Do not treat the old cooked roasted thigh draft as an active canonical wholefood entry.
